gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (173/ 8)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (173/ 8)
text: قَالَ لِي أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) يَا بَحْرُ حُسْنُ الْخُلُقِ يُسْرٌ ثُمَّ قَالَ أَ لَا أُخْبِرُكَ بِحَدِيثٍ مَا هُوَ فِي يَدَيْ أَحَدٍ مِنْ أَهْلِ الْمَدِينَةِ قُلْتُ بَلَى

قَالَ بَيْنَا رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) ذَاتَ يَوْمٍ جَالِسٌ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ إِذْ جَاءَتْ جَارِيَةٌ لِبَعْضِ الْأَنْصَارِ وَ هُوَ قَائِمٌ فَأَخَذَتْ بِطَرَفِ ثَوْبِهِ فَقَامَ لَهَا النَّبِيُّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) فَلَمْ تَقُلْ شَيْئاً وَ لَمْ يَقُلْ لَهَا النَّبِيُّ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) شَيْئاً حَتَّى فَعَلَتْ ذَلِكَ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ

فَقَامَ لَهَا النَّبِيُّ فِي الرَّابِعَةِ وَ هِيَ خَلْفَهُ فَأَخَذَتْ هُدْبَةً مِنْ ثَوْبِهِ ثُمَّ رَجَعَتْ فَقَالَ لَهَا النَّاسُ فَعَلَ اللَّهُ بِكِ وَ فَعَلَ حَبَسْتِ رَسُولَ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ لَا تَقُولِينَ لَهُ شَيْئاً وَ لَا هُوَ يَقُولُ لَكِ شَيْئاً مَا كَانَتْ حَاجَتُكِ إِلَيْهِ

قَالَتْ إِنَّ لَنَا مَرِيضاً فَأَرْسَلَنِي أَهْلِي لآِخُذَ هُدْبَةً مِنْ ثَوْبِهِ لِيَسْتَشْفِيَ بِهَا فَلَمَّا أَرَدْتُ أَخْذَهَا رَآنِي فَقَامَ فَاسْتَحْيَيْتُ مِنْهُ أَنْ آخُذَهَا وَ هُوَ يَرَانِي وَ أَكْرَهُ أَنْ أَسْتَأْمِرَهُ فِي أَخْذِهَا فَأَخَذْتُهَا .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Abu Abdullah, from his father, from Hammad Bin Isa, from Hareyz Bin Abdullah, from Bahr Al Saqqa who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said to me: ‘O Bahr! Good manners are an affluence’. Then he<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘Shall I<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> inform you with a Hadeeth which is not in the hands of anyone from the people of Al-Medina?’ I said, ‘Yes’.

He<sup style="font-weight: normal">asws</sup> said: ‘One day, while Rasool-Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> was seated in the Masjid when a slave girl of one of the Helpers came over, and he<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> was sitting upright. So she grabbed a side of his<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> clothes. So the Prophet<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> stood up for her, but she did not say anything, and the Prophet<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> did not say anything to her, to the extent that she did that three times.

So the Prophet<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> stood up for her during the fourth attempt, and she was behind him<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>, so she took an edge of his<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> cloth, then retracted. So the people said to her, ‘May Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> Deal with you and Do (whatever He<sup style="font-weight: normal">azwj</sup> may)! You withheld Rasool-Allah<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> three times, neither did you say anything to him nor did he<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> say anything to you. What was your need to him<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup>?’

She said, ‘For us there is a sick one. So my people sent me to take an edge from his<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> cloth in order to heal him by it. So whenever I wanted to take it, he<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> saw me, and stood up. So I was too embarrased from him<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> to take it while he<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> sees me, and I disliked to demand him<sup style="font-weight: normal">saww</sup> with regards to taking it, therefore I took it’.
en.sarwar: 15. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn abu ‘Abd Allah from his father from Hammad ibn ‘Isa from Hariz ibn ‘Abd Allah from Bahr al-Saqqa who has said the following: “Abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) said to me, ‘O Bahr, excellent moral behavior is prosperity. Would you like me to teach you a Hadith which is not found in the hands of any of the people of Madina?’ I said, ‘Yes, please do so.’ The Imam said, ‘Once the Messenger of Allah was in the Mosque when a girl from the people of Ansar came in and he was standing there. She held to one end of his (the Messenger of Allah’s) cloth, the Holy Prophet stood up. She did not say anything and he (the Holy Prophet) also did not say anything to her (he then sat down). She repeated it three times and at the fourth time the Holy Prophet stood up for her and she was behind him. She then took a piece from his cloth and returned back. People rebuked her saying, “May Allah do to you what He may decide to do. You held the Messenger of Allah as your captive. You did it three times. You said nothing to him nor did he say anything to you. What did you want from him?” She then said, “We had a patient and our people sent me to get a piece from his (the Messenger of Allah’s) cloth to be used to cure the patient. When I wanted to get the piece he saw me and stood up. I then felt shy to take what I wanted when he would see me. I did not like to demand anything from him. At last I took what I wanted to get.’””
